channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida woman who survived parents' murders as toddler reveals 'missing piece' after killer's execution
item.description: A woman who survived her parents' murders as a toddler reveals her "missing piece" after their killer's execution and her childhood memories connected to the horrific crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Killer nightclub hostess's deadly love affair with race car driver was 'eye for an eye': author
item.description: "A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story," a four-part drama series, was inspired by Carol Ann Lee's true-crime book, "A Fine Day for a Hanging: The Real Ruth Ellis Story.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is being buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group is scheduled to be buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 shot and killed outside Louisville, Kentucky, motor vehicle office, police say
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Three people were shot and killed outside a motor vehicle office in Louisville on Friday, police said.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration reverses its previous decision and reinstates legal aid for migrant children
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Days after telling legal groups who help migrant children who arrive in America alone — some so young they are in diapers or their feet dangle from their chairs in court — that they must stop their work, the federal government Friday reversed itself.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP sues 3 Trump administration officials, citing freedom of speech
item.description: The Associated Press is suing three Trump administration officials over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USPS reveals new 'American Vistas' postage stamps
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service revealed two new designs for Presorted Standard stamps in San Diego Friday, illustrating America's iconic natural landscapes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following a press conference with other Proud Boys members and Oath Keepers’ founder Stewart Rhodes.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA district attorney opposes new trial for Menendez brothers, still deciding on resentencing
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney says he not yet decided whether to recommend resentencing Lyle and Erik Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SS United States to embark on final voyage to Florida
item.description: A historic ocean liner departed on Thursday for her final voyage, where it heads towards its watery grave off of Florida's coast.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, Fox 5

item.title: ICE, Homeland Security raid bakery, charge owners with harboring illegal immigrants
item.description: Bakery owners in Texas are facing felony charges after ICE and HSI arrested eight suspected illegal immigrants employed by the shop.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Bodycam footage shows chaotic moments as Lawrence police officers punch, kick man during arrest
item.description: LAWRENCE, Ind. -- The Lawrence Police Department has released bodycam footage detailing the moments that led up to an altercation between officers and a man they were trying to arrest this week. An LPD officer responded around 4:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How LA wildfires are making an already tough rental market even worse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The one-bedroom cottage with a woodsy vibe reminded Heather McAlpine of the home she lost to the brutal Los Angeles-area wildfires. But only two hours after seeing the listing, the rental was snapped up.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted of criminally negligent homicide Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers beginning this week likely mean the end of the agency's plan to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections, experts say.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Unions sue over Trump admin efforts to fire probationary federal employees
item.description: A coalition of government employee unions sued late Wednesday over the Trump administration’s efforts to fire employees still in their probationary period across the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
